message:
feat(benches): add restart scheduling benchmark suite (wa-lr93)

Add Criterion benchmark targets for the survival module's restart
scheduling engine, measuring the three hot paths that gate automatic
restart decisions:

Benchmarks:
- scheduling_decision_latency: evaluate RestartScheduler::recommend()
  over 1,440 candidate one-minute windows (a full 24-hour sweep),
  measuring the end-to-end cost of scoring every possible restart slot
  including activity profile lookup and hazard urgency computation.
  Budget: p50 < 1ms.

- activity_profile_update: measure ActivityProfile::record_activity()
  for a single hourly EWMA bucket update.  Budget: p50 < 10us.

- hazard_urgency_computation: compute 100 urgency scores from
  HazardForecastPoint inputs with varying probability/impact pairs.
  Budget: p50 < 50us total.

Cargo.toml:
- Register [[bench]] name = "restart_scheduling", harness = false

The benchmarks construct realistic ActivityProfile state (24 hours of
synthetic activity) and RestartSchedulerConfig defaults, then exercise
the core scheduling decision path to detect regressions in the sigmoid
hazard scoring or EWMA window selection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -153,6 +153,10 @@ harness = false
 name = "snapshot_engine"
 harness = false
 
+[[bench]]
+name = "restart_scheduling"
+harness = false
+
 [[bench]]
 name = "pool_benchmark"
 harness = false
